title: Method, system and device for improved area and object illumination
abstract: The present invention relates to the field of illumination of object, in the widest sense it is ranging from small photo objects to large fields such as sports fields including but not limited to football stadium and cricket field, and also other environments in need of illumination, such as road tunnels. Creating or avoiding shadows and optimize the illumination of objects or surfaces is tedious task and often include the use of multiple light sources, which requires a relatively large space and it is not always practically achievable. The present invention solve this problem and other issues regarding illumination of objects and surfaces by provide a matrix mirror assembly comprising a illumination source and a matrix of individually controllable mirrors connected to a mirror matrix backplane (22) via mirror positioning means (13).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A mirror matrix assembly for illumination of an object or surface, c h a r a c t e r i z e d b y comprising: one or more mirrors (11, 240) connected to a mirror matrix backplane (22, 252) via mirror positioning means (13, 16, 81, 82, 250); one or more light sources (20, 44, 251), wherein the light sources are positioned for unobstructed illumination of one or more of the one or more mirrors (11, 240) in the mirror matrix (10), and the mirrors (11, 240) are positioned to reflect the light from the one or more light sources and illuminate an object or surface. 2. The mirror matrix assembly of claim 1, wherein: the mirror matrix assembly further comprise a light mast (243), wherein the one or more light sources (20, 44, 251) is arranged in a lamp house (241) in the lower part of the light mast (243), the light sources (20, 44, 251) pointing upwards, and the one or more mirrors (11, 240) connected to a mirror matrix backplane (22, 252) is arranged in a mirror housing (246) in the top part of the light mast (243), the mirror arranged to have its front sides mainly downwards. 3. The mirror matrix assembly of claim 1, wherein: the positioning means (13, 16, 81, 82, 250) is composed of one or more actuators (250) arranged typically in a matrix pattern connected to a backplane (252) of the mirror housing (246) in one distal end, and in the other distal end being affixed to the backside of the mirror (240). 4. The mirror matrix assembly of claim 1, wherein: the mirror matrix assembly further comprise a controller (247) comprising computing means, the controller being connected to the one or more actuators (250) and/or the light sources (20, 44, 251) for controlling their individual movement. 5. The mirror matrix assembly of claim 3 or 4, wherein: 

 the actuators is of one of pneumatic actuators, electromotor actuator, spindle actuators, or magnetic actuator. 6. The mirror matrix assembly of claim 1, wherein: one or more of the one or more light sources (20, 44) are arranged to be directed away from the object or surface to be illuminated by the reflected light, from the one or more light sources (20, 44), of the one or more mirrors (11). 7. The mirror matrix assembly of claim 1, wherein: one or more of the one or more light sources (20, 44) are arranged to be directed towards the object or surface to be illuminated by the reflected light, from the one or more light sources (20, 44), of the one or more mirrors (11). 8. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims, wherein: the mirror positioning means (13, 16, 81, 82) provides individual adjustment of each mirror (11) in the mirror matrix (10). 9. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims, wherein: the mirror positioning means (13, 16, 81, 82) further enables rotation of individual mirrors around multiple axis. 10. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims, wherein: the mirror positioning means (13, 16, 81, 82) comprises a ball (18) fastened to the mirror matrix backplane (22), wherein the ball (18) is made of a material responsive to a magnetic force, and a fastening means for the mirror (11) comprising a ring (17), the ring being of a magnetic material, and the inside of the ring being formatted to fit around a part of the ball (18) which is not at the side fastened to the mirror matrix backplane (22), such that the mirror (11) can be moved in any position around and attached by magnetic force to the free side of the ball (18). 11. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims 1 to 9 wherein: the mirror positioning means (13, 16, 81, 82, 250) comprises one or more motors in a motor assembly (13, 81, 82) wherein the motors are able to position the mirror in any angle within an adjustment range. 12. The mirror matrix assembly of claim 11, wherein: mirror matrix assembly further comprising a computer resource (104) comprising a control program, and each motor assembly (13, 81, 82) comprising a controller (12) being controlled by the control program for controlling the operation of the motors. 13. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims, wherein: one or more of the one or more light sources (20, 44, 251) are arranged in a fixed position relative the mirror matrix assembly. 14. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims, wherein: one or more of the one or more light sources (20, 44, 251) are arranged in a controllable position relative the mirror matrix assembly, such that by varying the distance between the light source and the mirrors can alter the softness of the illumination of the object or surface to be illuminated by the reflected light, from the one or more light sources (20, 44, 251), of the one or more mirrors (11, 240). 15. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims, wherein: each mirror (11, 240) may be comprised of a mirror having a unique configuration comprising one or more of: form, curvature, color or motive. 16. The mirror matrix assembly of any of the previous claims, wherein: the mirrors being plasma mirrors being electrically controlled and the mirror matrix assembly further comprising a computer resource (104) comprising a control program for controlling the plasma mirrors. 17. A system for illumination of an object or surface comprising a mirror matrix assembly as defined in any of the previous claims, a computing means (101, 104), communication path (102) between computing means (104, 101) and one or more mirror matrix assemblies. 18. A system according to claim 17, further comprising a cloud or internet service (100) wherein the computing means (104, 101) are connected to the one or more mirror matrix assemblies via the cloud or internet service (100).
